text:
Once upon a twilight hour in sleepy Oakdale, there lived a wise old owl. This owl had learned many things. He knew that every cloud had a silver lining. He also knew that gossip spread faster than an owl swooping down.

One day a peculiar fox came into town carrying an unusual-looking book. It was big and purple. "What curious thing is this?" the fox asked everyone.

"A cookbook!" exclaimed a hawk. "You read that?"

"I sure do," said the fox. "And I'll share my famous pumpkin pie." He whisked some ingredients together and baked himself a pie. It was sweet.

Word spread. People liked the pie. The fox got famous. Once word got out about an exclusive recipe, more foxes brought pies. Soon trees were draped in them.

The wise owl thought about it. Owls couldn't eat pies. They prefer worms. So he didn't tell everyone. Perhaps some adventures deserved secrets. He'd look forward to that pie on
